market_context: Futures are down 0.4% ahead of April CPI release at 8:30 AM, with economists expecting 3.7-3.8% headline inflation driven by energy costs. Oil remains elevated at $100/barrel amid U.S.-Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz closure.

predictions.reasoning: Hot CPI print of 3.7-3.8% expected due to energy price surge will likely trigger risk-off sentiment and concern about persistent inflation. Fed rate cut expectations will be pushed further out, pressuring equities despite recent strength.

Already down 4.3% in premarket on profit-taking after 500% run-up. Trading at extreme 119x forward P/E with Apple foundry talks still unconfirmed. High RSI and parabolic move create asymmetric downside risk on any disappointment.

Oil at $100+ with Strait of Hormuz effectively closed and no clear resolution to U.S.-Iran conflict. Energy sector should outperform on continued supply disruption fears and inflation data confirming energy price impact.

Hot CPI will reinforce Fed hawkishness and push rate cut expectations further into 2027. Higher inflation expectations will pressure long-duration bonds despite any flight-to-quality bid from geopolitical tensions.

CPI surprise risk combined with ongoing Middle East tensions and uncertain Iran peace negotiations should drive volatility higher. Current VIX level appears complacent given multiple risk factors converging today.
